article_title: America's Open Banking Deadline Arrives to an Empty Room as Courts and the Trump Administration Leave Section 1033 in Limbo

content_preview.title: America's Open Banking Deadline Arrives to an Empty Room as Courts and the Trump Administration Leave Section 1033 in Limbo
content_preview.summary: The CFPB's April 1 compliance deadline for its landmark Personal Financial Data Rights rule passed without enforcement after a federal judge enjoined the regulation and the agency itself declared it unlawful, leaving the future of U.S. open banking to a market moving faster than its regulators.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## A Deadline Without a Rule

April 1, 2026, was supposed to be a landmark date in American consumer finance. The Consumer Financial Protection Bureau's Personal Financial Data Rights rule -- built on Section 1033 of the Dodd-Frank Act -- required the nation's largest banks, those holding at least $250 billion in assets, to begin sharing customer financial data with authorized third parties through standardized APIs at no charge to consumers.
